title: Sodium benzoate (E211) as a preservative
summary: Sodium benzoate (E211) is permitted in limited amounts but can irritate sensitive individuals and is generally unnecessary in many drinks.
recommendation: Prefer beverages without E211 when possible.
Check the ingredient list for E-numbers like E211.

title: Monosodium glutamate (MSG, E621)
summary: MSG (E621) enhances flavour but is not essential. Some children may be sensitive. Processed snacks and instant noodles often contain it.
recommendation: Choose whole foods and simple ingredient lists for kids.

title: Artificial colours in sweets and snacks (E102, E110, E122)
summary: Certain artificial colours (like E102 Tartrazine, E110 Sunset Yellow, E122 Azorubine) may cause reactions in sensitive children.
recommendation: Choose products without bright artificial colours.
Check for E-numbers on labels and prefer natural colours.

title: High added sugar in packaged foods
summary: Many packaged foods and drinks contain high added sugar. Checking per 100g/100ml helps compare options.
recommendation: Pick options with less added sugar per 100g/100ml.
Prefer whole and minimally processed snacks.

title: High sodium in savoury snacks and instant noodles
summary: Instant noodles and savoury snacks can be high in sodium, which is not ideal for young children.
recommendation: Limit high-sodium snacks, especially for toddlers.
Compare sodium per 100g and choose lower-salt options.

title: Energy drinks are not for children
summary: Energy drinks with caffeine and additives are not recommended for children.
recommendation: Offer water, milk, or fresh homemade beverages instead.

title: Infant foods and labelling (FSSAI)
summary: Infant foods have specific FSSAI standards and labelling. Parents should follow age-appropriate guidance.
